result.markdown:
--- title: "Functional connectivity" subtitle: "Seed-to-target correlations and Fisher z transforms." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Seed-Based Correlations Functional connectivity analyses often start from a seed time series and a matrix of target time series. This page keeps the calculation explicit and uses `fmrimod` for common effect-size transforms. ::: {#8c9cde5c .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.residualize import residualize rng = np.random.default_rng(13) n_time = 160 n_targets = 8 seed = rng.normal(size=n_time) targets = rng.normal(size=(n_time, n_targets)) targets[:, :3] += 0.45 * seed[:, None] corr = np.array([np.corrcoef(seed, targets[:, j])[0, 1] for j in range(n_targets)]) z, z_var = fm.r_to_z(corr, n_time) pd.DataFrame({ "target": [f"roi_{j + 1}" for j in range(n_targets)], "r": corr, "fisher_z": z, "z_variance": z_var, }).round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
target r fisher_z z_variance
0 roi_1 0.587 0.673 0.006
1 roi_2 0.373 0.392 0.006
2 roi_3 0.488 0.534 0.006
3 roi_4 0.003 0.003 0.006
4 roi_5 0.094 0.095 0.006
5 roi_6 -0.017 -0.017 0.006
6 roi_7 -0.063 -0.063 0.006
7 roi_8 -0.007 -0.007 0.006
``` ::: ::: ## Return to Correlation Units Fisher z values are useful for group summaries because they are closer to normal. Convert back to correlations for presentation. ::: {#4fed2ace .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} pooled_z = z.mean() pooled_r = fm.z_to_r(pooled_z) round(float(pooled_z), 3), round(float(pooled_r), 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` (0.201, 0.199) ``` ::: ::: ## Remove a Nuisance Signal A common preprocessing step is residualizing the seed and target signals with respect to nuisance columns before correlation. ::: {#f66f880a .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} nuisance = np.column_stack([np.ones(n_time), np.linspace(-1, 1, n_time)]) seed_resid = residualize(nuisance, seed[:, None]).ravel() target_resid = residualize(nuisance, targets) partial_corr = np.array([ np.corrcoef(seed_resid, target_resid[:, j])[0, 1] for j in range(n_targets) ]) pd.DataFrame({"raw_r": corr, "residualized_r": partial_corr}).round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
raw_r residualized_r
0 0.587 0.589
1 0.373 0.373
2 0.488 0.488
3 0.003 0.004
4 0.094 0.094
5 -0.017 -0.017
6 -0.063 -0.064
7 -0.007 -0.006
``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd) - [Datasets](datasets.qmd)
